name: Googlesheets Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Googlesheets Workflow. This workflow integrates 11 different services: filter, stickyNote, code, scheduleTrigger, set. It contains 34 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note8

parameters.content:
# Try me out
1. Clone [this Google worksheet]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and update the 'Settings' node with its URL (plus change any other settings there you need to)
2. Adjust the sequence of emails you want to send in the 'Email Sequence' node

Make sure you set how many days after the previous email to wait before sending, and note that the emails are in HTML format.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.jsonOutput:
{ "emails": [ { "message": "Hi {name}, hope you're well!

You're doing some great things at {company}, and I wanted to touch base to see if you wanted to chat.

Would it make sense to jump on a quick call?

Regards,

Nathan
", "send_on_day": 0 }, { "message": "Hi {name},

Just wanted to follow up on this, since it would be great to talk.

Regards,

Nathan
", "send_on_day": 3 }, { "message": "Just thought I'd give this one last try :)

Nathan ", "send_on_day": 8 } ] }

parameters.content:
Follow-up is due if:
- All the messages in the thread are automated (no-one has replied yet)
- Enough time has passed for the next message to be sent

If reply_message_id is set, will reply to that message.

Otherwise, will send a new message to the user in the 'email' field

Columns the sheet needs
- email
- first_emailed (leave blank - will be filled in automatically)
- Other columns matching placeholders in email sequence

## Sub-workflow for sending the emails
This is called by the sections above - you shouldn't need to touch it

# Try me out
1. Clone [this Google worksheet]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and update the 'Settings' node with its URL (plus change any other settings there you need to)
2. Adjust the sequence of emails you want to send in the 'Email Sequence' node

Make sure you set how many days after the previous email to wait before sending, and note that the emails are in HTML format.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Execute Workflow Trigger".
Open the scheduler node "Every hour".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Update last contacted time" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Get previous message threads" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get thread details" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Next message due?" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Replying?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send new message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Call message send sub-workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Call message send sub-workflow1" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "To email?" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Reply to message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get emails" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Don't email on weekends" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set message template".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Email sequence".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Compose message".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Prepare reply params".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Prepare first message params".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Settings".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Incoming triggers will qualified/formatted, new rows will be written to Google Sheets, and email replies or alerts will be sent automatically.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
